reference: I am a chronic immune thrombocytopenic purpura patient since birth. I am now fifty-one years old and male, having creatinine level of nine and urea level of two hundred and forty. I do not want to undergo dialysis because of my low platelet count, as complications may increase. I am nervous, though slowly the creatinine level is coming down. Should I avoid dialysis?
text: I have tried to meet with the company, but I am not the only one who has reacted negatively to it. I am not opposed to the plan, as competition is always good. I am not the sole reactor, if I put it that way.
